parentQuestion: Specific motor and sensory functions are localized in specific areas called doma...

scaffolds.q: The brain has different regions that handle different functions.
scaffolds.explain: The brain divides labor with ruthless efficiency. Vision gets the occipital lobe. Hearing gets temporal real estate. Movement gets the motor strip. Different regions, different jobs — organizational genius born from evolutionary pressure.

scaffolds.q: Which part of the brain controls voluntary movement?
scaffolds.explain: The motor cortex — that strip of frontal lobe tissue right behind your forehead — sends the 'go' signals. Every voluntary twitch starts here. Your toe? Mapped. Your tongue? Mapped. Even your eyelids get their own address on this neural switchboard.
